title: calogyne pilosa subsp. chinensis
intro: Calogyne pilosa subsp. chinensis, also known as the Chinese hairy calogyne, is a plant species within the family Bignoniaceae, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Calogyne pilosa subsp. chinensis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit immunomodulatory effects, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Calogyne pilosa subsp. chinensis include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to create teas, tonics, and supplements for therapeutic purposes.
